faults.name: Load resistance is comparable to the lower divider resistor
faults.why: A low-resistance load appears in parallel with R2 and pulls the divider output below the no-load expectation.
faults.verification_test: Measure Vout with and without the load connected.
faults.fix_recipe: Use a buffer, increase divider current, or raise the load resistance.

faults.name: One divider resistor has the wrong value
faults.why: A swapped decade or wrong color-code band shifts the divider ratio.
faults.verification_test: Power off and measure R1 and R2 directly.
faults.fix_recipe: Replace the incorrect resistor and recompute Vout = Vin*R2/(R1+R2).

faults.name: Lower resistor ground is open
faults.why: If R2 does not return to ground, the tap voltage is undefined under load.
faults.verification_test: Check continuity from the lower resistor to ground.
faults.fix_recipe: Reconnect R2 to the common reference node.
